title: Authorization, Tenant Isolation, and Abuse Resistance · Mastery Review
summary: A review board compares near-miss decisions for authorization, tenant isolation, and abuse resistance and accepts only observable behavior, human evidence, explicit non-claims, and owned transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for deny-by-default authorization and abuse case suite. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose role, attribute, relationship, capability, or policy models from resource, action, subject, context, and review needs.
Enforce authorization at every trusted resource and transition boundary using current server-side state.
Propagate tenant identity through query, cache, file, job, event, log, backup, restore, and support paths with adversarial tests.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-2 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-2
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Privileged Operation
steps.instruction:
quiz case — An authenticated member changes an identifier, crosses organization data, automates an expensive action, and exploits a standing administrator role. The quiz variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Require least privilege, just-in-time or bounded elevation, reason, approval where needed, audit, notification, and revocation for privileged work.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Privileged Operation.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for require least privilege, just-in-time or bounded elevation, reason, approval where needed, audit, notification, and revocation for privileged work. For Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Privileged Operation, keep Privileged Operation connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: An authenticated member changes an identifier, crosses organization data, automates an expensive action, and exploits a standing administrator role. The quiz variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Require least privilege, just-in-time or bounded elevation, reason, approval where needed, audit, notification, and revocation for privileged work.

content.title: Fault-isolation decision
content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the Privileged Operation symptom in deny-by-default authorization and abuse case suite during this quiz case.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: NIST Secure Software Development Framework — Controls prepared organizations, protected software, well-secured production, vulnerability response, provenance, verification, and release evidence.

hints: For Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Privileged Operation, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Privileged Operation precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Privileged Operation, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: An authenticated member changes an identifier, crosses organization data, automates an expensive action, and exploits a standing administrator role. The quiz variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Require least privilege, just-in-time or bounded elevation, reason, approval where needed, audit, notification, and revocation for privileged work.

For Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Privileged Operation, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Administrators should have unrestricted standing access.

stimulus.title: Privileged Operation failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Administrators should have unrestricted standing access.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Require least privilege, just-in-time or bounded elevation, reason, approval where needed, audit, notification, and revocation for privileged work.

options.text: For diagnose the hidden failure, apply Privileged Operation without its stated preconditions, omit the deny-by-default authorization and abuse case suite trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Privileged Operation for deny-by-default authorization and abuse case suite.
Run the Privileged Operation boundary case for deny-by-default authorization and abuse case suite, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

Accept the Privileged Operation result because visible deny-by-default authorization and abuse case suite output is the highest authority. That claim fails the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary for Privileged Operation.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Privileged Operation
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Privileged Operation: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — An authenticated member changes an identifier, crosses organization data, automates an expensive action, and exploits a standing administrator role. The quiz variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Require least privilege, just-in-time or bounded elevation, reason, approval where needed, audit, notification, and revocation for privileged work.

Evidence target: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Privileged Operation.
steps.why: Retrieving Privileged Operation through the concrete deny-by-default authorization and abuse case suite case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Privileged Operation, keep Privileged Operation connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Require least privilege, just-in-time or bounded elevation, reason, approval where needed, audit, notification, and revocation for privileged work.
